card.intuition: A query language for your API that allows clients to request exactly the data they need and nothing more.
card.analogy: Ordering from a bespoke sandwich shop: you tell the chef exactly what toppings you want, instead of picking from a pre-set list of sandwiches where you might have to pick off the onions.
when_to_use: Solving under-fetching (multiple REST calls for one view) and over-fetching (getting too many fields)
When the frontend needs highly flexible, nested data structures
Consolidating multiple microservices into a single graph endpoint
gotchas: N+1 problem: poorly written resolvers can trigger many database queries for one GraphQL query
Caching is harder than REST because every request is a POST to the same URL

exercise.prompt: Define a GraphQL schema for a User and its posts.
hints: A user's name is usually a String
The User has an array of Posts
The second type name is Post
exercise.test_code:
assert "type User" in typedefs
assert "posts: [Post]" in typedefs
assert "type Post" in typedefs
print("PASS")
